Off Calendar Feb-05-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. BRYAN J. McCORMACK, Esq. of McCormack Law Firm (Tel: 415-925-5161) for Plaintiff Nathaniel Dwyer;
Atty. MEL M.C. COLE, Esq. (Tel: 415-276-2522) and MARGARET L. WATSON, Esq. (Tel: 212-471-4492) of Littler Mendelson P.C. for Defendants Alvarez & Marsal Holdings, LLC and Alvarez & Marsal Corporate Performance Improvement, LLC.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS A&M CORPORATE PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT LLC AND ALVAREZ & MARSAL HOLDINGS, LLC's Motion To Stay Action Pending Arbitration.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants' "motion to stay action pending arbitration" is denied. The supposed confidentiality of certain defendant information is not an issue raised in plaintiff's operative first amended complaint. (See 9 U.S.C. Section 3; CCP Section 1281.4.) Nor have defendants filed a cross-claim regarding that issue. Moreover, California has extensive statutes permitting protective orders and sealing of confidential information in court proceedings.

Order signed.

There being no objection by plaintiff, the Court will permit defendants to submit a proposed order regarding the sealing of document that defendants accidentally submitted as a public document which contains confidential information.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
